LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLARIFY THE ALLEGATIONS OF PARAGRAPHS 14 AND 15 SO AS TO DETERMINE WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAS A CLAIM UNDER COMMERCIAL CODE SECTION 3405. THE COURT CONTINUES TO HOLD THAT THE CALIFORNIA COMMERCIAL CODE, NOT COMMON LAW, IS THE ONLY APPARENT BASIS FOR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. THE COURT PREVIOUSLY ADDRESSED, AND DECLINED TO ORDER, PLAINTIFF TO SUE THE BANK OR BANK(S). THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT SUBJECT TO DISMISSAL ON STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS GROUNDS. EACH CHECK WOULD APPEAR TO BE A SEPARATE WRONG. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER APPROVED TO FORM. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents (Set One); Memo Of P And A ; Declaration
Off Calendar Jul-12-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Jean A. Hobart, Esq., appearing for Defendant & moving party: Neiman Marcus
Patrick Clayton, Esq., appearing for plaintiff & opposing party: Brian P. Burns

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Neiman Marcus Group, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to Numbers 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14. The objections are overruled.
The motion is denied as to No. 20. The request is vague and ambiguous and overly broad.
The motion is granted as to No. 7, redacting payees and amounts. The objections are overruled.
The motion is denied as to Numbers 15, and 16 as overly broad.
The motion is denied as to No. 21 as it is moot.
The motion is denied as to No. 22. The response is adequate.
The motion is denied as to No. 23 as it is overly broad.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Defendant The Neiman Marcus Group, Inc.'S Demurrer To First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-23-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: DANIEL S. MASON, ESQUIRE (ZELLE HOFMANN VOELBEL MASON & GETTE LLP, 415.693.0700) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JEAN A. HOBART, ESQUIRE (TUCKER ELLIS & WEST LLP, 213.430.3365) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLARIFY THE ALLEGATIONS OF PARAGRAPHS 14 AND 15 SO AS TO DETERMINE WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAS A CLAIM UNDER COMMERCIAL CODE SECTION 3405. THE COURT CONTINUES TO HOLD THAT THE CALIFORNIA COMMERCIAL CODE, NOT COMMON LAW, IS THE ONLY APPARENT BASIS FOR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. THE COURT PREVIOUSLY ADDRESSED, AND DECLINED TO ORDER, PLAINTIFF TO SUE THE BANK OR BANK(S). THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT SUBJECT TO DISMISSAL ON STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS GROUNDS. EACH CHECK WOULD APPEAR TO BE A SEPARATE WRONG. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER APPROVED TO FORM. (302/PJM/PM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Or Modify Subpoena
Off Calendar May-22-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Patrick Clayton, Esq., for plaintiff & moving party: Brian P. Burns
Jean A. Hobart, Esq., for Defendant & opposing party: The Neiman Marcus Group

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Brian P. Burn's Motion To Quash Or Modify Subpoena is granted as it seeks to quash the subpoena. The subpoena is overly broad. The summary of information sought as set forth in the opposition papers is close to the limitations that must be adopted. Defendant also must refrain from using Plaintiff's unredacted social security number. Counsel are ordered to meet and confer regarding the proper scope of the subpoena. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
